message:
perf(scheduler): add #[inline] hints to hot-path scheduling and data structures

Annotate small, frequently-called methods on the scheduler hot path
with #[inline] to help the compiler eliminate call overhead and enable
cross-crate inlining for these tight loops.

Scheduler data structures:
- global_injector.rs: inject_cancel, inject_ready, pop_cancel, pop_ready
- global_queue.rs: new, push, pop, len, is_empty
- intrusive.rs: IntrusiveRing push_back/pop_front/remove,
  IntrusiveStack push/pop/steal_one
- intrusive_heap.rs: push, pop
- local_queue.rs: push, push_many, pop, is_empty, with_tasks_arena_mut
- priority.rs: schedule, schedule_cancel, pop

Sync primitives:
- contended_mutex.rs: new, lock, Deref::deref, DerefMut::deref_mut
  (the non-instrumented inner impl that wraps std::sync::Mutex)

Core utilities:
- arena.rs: insert, get, get_mut (the most frequently called Arena
  methods, used on every task schedule/poll/complete cycle)

These methods are all small (< 20 lines), called per-task-poll, and
sit behind trait boundaries or module walls that prevent the compiler
from inlining without the hint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
